steps.title: Expand and establish constraints
steps.content:
Expand $(3x^2 + a)(2x^2 + b)$: $$6x^4 + 3bx^2 + 2ax^2 + ab = 6x^4 + (3b+2a)x^2 + ab$$
Match this with $6x^4 + 19x^2 + 8$ to get two conditions:
  • Coefficient of $x^2$: $3b + 2a = 19$
  • Constant term: $ab = 8$

steps.title: Find all solutions via quadratic formula
steps.content:
From $ab = 8$, we have $b = \frac{8}{a}$. Substitute into $3b + 2a = 19$: $$\frac{24}{a} + 2a = 19$$
Multiply by $a$ and rearrange: $$2a^2 - 19a + 24 = 0$$
By the quadratic formula: $$a = \frac{19 \pm \sqrt{361 - 192}}{4} = \frac{19 \pm \sqrt{169}}{4} = \frac{19 \pm 13}{4}$$
So $a = \frac{32}{4} = 8$ (the integer solution) or $a = \frac{6}{4} = \frac{3}{2}$ (the non-integer solution).
Therefore, $a = 8$ and $c = \frac{3}{2}$.
